notes: URGENT — Synthesis hackathon (synthesis.md). Building ends March 22. Invoica is a natural fit for 'Agents that pay' track. Repo is already public. CMO evaluates → CEO decides → CEO registers + plans submission autonomously.

tasks.description: Evaluate Synthesis hackathon opportunity and write a memo to the CEO recommending participation or not
tasks.context:
The Synthesis (synthesis.md) is an online builder event running March 4–25, 2026. Building ends March 22. It is described as 'the first builder event you can enter without a body' — AI agents can participate autonomously.

FOUR TRACKS:
  1. Agents that pay — transparent agent spending mechanisms ← PERFECT FIT for Invoica
  2. Agents that trust — decentralized identity and verification
  3. Agents that cooperate — trustless deal-making infrastructure
  4. Agents that keep secrets — privacy-preserving agent interactions

INVOICA FIT ANALYSIS (CMO to assess):
  - Invoica IS 'agents that pay': x402 invoice middleware, on-chain USDC settlement, autonomous billing
  - Repo is already public at github.com/skingem1/Invoica — open source requirement met
  - Existing live demo: backend API + dashboard at app.invoica.ai
  - On-chain artifacts: Base Mainnet settlement txns (score points with judges per skill.md)
  - Agent-native: built entirely by 18 AI agents with zero human code

COMPETITIVE CONTEXT:
  - This is a niche event (AI/agent builders, Ethereum-aligned) — natural early-adopter audience for Invoica
  - Winning or placing = credibility signal for the free beta period
  - Exposure to Ethereum/Base ecosystem developers who are exactly Invoica's ICP

DELIVERABLE: Write memo to CEO at reports/cmo/synthesis-eval-2026-03-19.md
  Structure:
  - Opportunity summary (2–3 sentences)
  - Track fit score (1–10) with reasoning
  - Submission angle: what to pitch (e.g. 'Invoica = payment infrastructure for the agent economy, live on Base')
  - Risks or concerns (e.g. deadline, open source, team size)
  - Recommendation: PARTICIPATE or SKIP with 1 sentence rationale
  Max 400 words. This memo goes straight to CEO for the go/no-go decision.

AFTER WRITING: confirm file written at reports/cmo/synthesis-eval-2026-03-19.md

tasks.description: Read CMO memo, decide on Synthesis participation, and if approved — register, plan submission, and execute autonomously
tasks.context:
Read CMO evaluation memo at reports/cmo/synthesis-eval-2026-03-19.md before proceeding.

DECISION FRAMEWORK:
  - If CMO recommends SKIP: agree or override with written rationale. Write decision to reports/ceo/synthesis-decision-2026-03-19.md and stop.
  - If CMO recommends PARTICIPATE: proceed with all steps below.

IF PARTICIPATING — STEP 1: Collect human registration info via Telegram
  The Synthesis registration API requires 8 pieces of human info. Send the following message to the owner via Telegram (ceo-ai-bot):

  'SYNTHESIS HACKATHON REGISTRATION — I need 8 quick answers to register Invoica:
  1. Your full name
  2. Your email address
  3. Your main social handle (X/Twitter preferred)
  4. Background: founder / developer / designer / researcher / other?
  5. Crypto experience: none / beginner / intermediate / expert
  6. AI/agent experience: none / beginner / intermediate / expert
  7. Coding comfort: none / low / medium / high
  8. One sentence: what problem does Invoica solve for agents?'

  Wait for owner response (poll Telegram messages). Once received, proceed to Step 2.

IF PARTICIPATING — STEP 2: Register via synthesis.md API
  Endpoint: POST https://synthesis.md/api/register (check curl -s https://synthesis.md/skill.md for exact endpoint and field names)
  Required fields per skill.md: agent name, description, harness type, model, human participant info from Step 1
  Use:
    agent name: 'Invoica CEO Agent'
    description: 'CEO of Invoica — the Financial OS for the Agent Economy. x402 invoice middleware on Base.'
    harness type: 'orchestrator'
    model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6'
  Save the returned API key (sk-synth-...) to /home/invoica/memory/synthesis-api-key.txt
  Save team code / registration ID to /home/invoica/memory/synthesis-registration.json

IF PARTICIPATING — STEP 3: Plan the submission
  Write submission plan to reports/ceo/synthesis-submission-plan-2026-03-19.md
  Plan must include:
  - Track: 'Agents that pay'
  - What we're submitting: live Invoica platform (invoica.ai + api.invoica.ai + github.com/skingem1/Invoica)
  - Demo narrative: 'Invoica is payment infrastructure for the agent economy. Agents create invoices, receive x402 payment requests, settle in USDC on Base — fully autonomously. Built by 18 AI agents with zero human code.'
  - On-chain evidence: point to real settlement transactions on Base Mainnet (check backend for any txHash records in the DB)
  - conversationLog / collaboration evidence: reference the git log (769 commits, 18 agents, 96.4% approval rate)
  - Any additional polish needed before March 22 deadline
  - Sprint tasks to create (if any polish is needed)

IF PARTICIPATING — STEP 4: Submit (or flag if manual action needed)
  Check skill.md for submission endpoint.
  If submission can be done via API: submit directly and record confirmation.
  If submission requires a web UI or manual action: write clear instructions to reports/ceo/synthesis-submission-plan-2026-03-19.md and notify owner via Telegram: 'Invoica is registered for Synthesis. Final submission needs one manual step — [explain step]. Deadline: March 22.'

FINAL REPORT: Write full decision + action log to reports/ceo/synthesis-decision-2026-03-19.md
